title: Tokyo “Grand van Gogh Exhibition: Terrace of a Café at Night” Admission E-Ticket
overview:
Van Gogh’s masterpiece Terrace of a Café at Night (Place du Forum) comes to Japan




Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890) — a painter beloved around the world. Although his career as an artist lasted only about 10 years, the many paintings and letters he left behind reveal a man who faced a life full of struggles and transformed them into art.
highlights: View Van Gogh’s masterpiece *Café Terrace at Night (Forum Square)*!
The exhibition focuses on the first half of Van Gogh’s life.
Purchase with your Rakuten account to earn and use Rakuten Points!
important_info:
• Tickets sold on this site are valid only from 2026-05-29 to 2026-06-30. Please note that they cannot be used for visits from 2026-07-01 onward.
• After purchasing a ticket on this site, we recommend making a free date-and-time reservation through the official online ticket system (etix). Without a reservation, you may be required to wait before entering.
Official website:
English: https://www.e-tix.jp/vangogh_tokyo/en
Japanese: https://www.e-tix.jp/vangogh_tokyo/
• Please present both your purchased e-ticket and your time-slot reservation (free) QR ticket upon arrival.
• Up to 10 tickets can be purchased per reservation.
• Visitors with a disability certificate and one accompanying caregiver receive a 50% discount. Please purchase these tickets at the venue ticket counter or through the official online ticket site (etix).
• Students and visitors with various certificates must present valid proof upon entry.
• Cancellations, ticket type changes, date/time changes, refunds, and reissues are not possible after purchase.
• Ticket sales methods and entry procedures may change. Please check the Tokyo exhibition’s official website for the latest information.
what_to_bring:
• Your e-ticket on a mobile device with internet connection (to redeem tickets, you must log in with the account used to book)
• Time-Specific Reservation QR Ticket (free)
After purchasing your e-ticket, please make a reservation for a specific date and time on the official online ticketing site. Japanese site: https://www.e-tix.jp/vangogh_tokyo/
meeting_point: [The Ueno Royal Museum] 1-2 Ueno Park, Taito Ward, Tokyo
schedule: Tickets purchased on this website are valid from May 29 to June 30, 2026. They cannot be used after July 1.
description:
One of the first people to recognize the value of Van Gogh (who received little recognition during his lifetime) and begin collecting his works was Helene Kröller-Müller (1869–1939), founder of the Kröller-Müller Museum in the Netherlands. The “Great Van Gogh Exhibition,” held in two phases, is composed entirely of works from this museum’s collection.




The first phase of the exhibition focuses on the early half of Van Gogh’s life. It begins with his early Dutch period influenced by the Barbizon School and the Hague School, continues through his time in Paris where he interacted with Impressionist painters, and culminates in Arles in southern France where he created the masterpiece Terrace of a Café at Night (Place du Forum).




The second phase, scheduled for 2027–2028, will feature The Langlois Bridge at Arles returning to Japan for the first time in approximately 70 years. The exhibition will tour Kobe, Fukushima, and Tokyo. The year 2025 marked 30 years since the Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake, and 2026 marks 15 years since the Great East Japan Earthquake. We sincerely hope that encountering Van Gogh’s works — often described as a “search of the soul” — will inspire visitors and serve as a source of strength for the future.
access:
The Ueno Royal Museum
1-2 Ueno Park, Taito Ward, Tokyo 110-0007




3-minute walk from JR Ueno Station (Park Exit)
5-minute walk from the Tokyo Metro or Keisei Electric Railway Ueno Station
how_it_works:
• Using tickets: your e-ticket voucher requires an internet connection to use, and should only be redeemed by staff (click here for how to use).
• Tickets can only be redeemed with the user account that booked them; please ensure you can access the same account on the day you visit.

title: Yokohama Belgian Beer Weekend 2026 [ 5/21 (Thu) - 5/24 (Sun) ] [E-Ticket]
overview:
【BBW2026 Yokohama】
Enjoy Belgian Beer Weekend Yokohama at Yamashita Park, right on the waterfront. Belgian beers, gourmet food, live music, and specialty booths come together in a refreshing seaside setting — a unique beer festival by the bay.




【The Belgian Beer Weekend】
The event made its debut in Japan in 2010 as a three-day festival welcoming 15,000 visitors. Today, the Belgian Beer Weekend is held in major metropolitan areas across Japan and has grown into one of the country’s leading outdoor events, attracting more than 1.8 million visitors over the past 16 years.




Yamashita Park
5/21 (Thu) - 5/24 (Sun)
Thursday 16:00-21:00
Friday & Saturday 11:00-21:00
Sunday 11:00-20:00
(last order 30min before closing time)
highlights: Earn & Use Rakuten Points
85+ Belgian Beers
Authentic Belgian Gourmet
Live Music
important_info:
When using your e-ticket, you will need to access the web page on your smartphone. Please ensure you have a stable internet connection. You must also log in using the account used at the time of booking in order to display your ticket, so please check in advance.
For instructions on how to use your e-ticket, please click here.
As a general rule, tickets cannot be cancelled, exchanged or refunded after purchase.
Presale tickets will only be refunded if the entire event (all scheduled event days) is cancelled. Refunds apply to the ticket price only.
Admission to the event is free. Charges apply for beer and food.
Beers and food can only be purchased with the original BBW2026 tokens. Cash payments are not accepted at beer and food booths.
Beer is served exclusively in the original BBW2026 Glass.
Extra tokens can be purchased at the event (1 token: ¥230).
The original BBW2026 Glass is yours to keep. During the event period, you may re-enter by bringing your glass and purchasing additional tokens.
Presale tickets are available until just before the event begins (May 21, 16:00).
After the event starts, tickets will be sold at the regular day ticket price.
[Alcohol consumption!]
Minors are welcome to enter, but as the consumption of alcohol by persons under the age of 20 is prohibited by law, we will not provide any alcoholic beverages. Please be aware that we may check your age at the entrance.
As this is an alcohol-related event, please refrain from driving a car, motorcycle, or bicycle to the event.
what_to_bring:
E-Ticket
To display your ticket, you must log in using the account used at the time of booking. Please make sure you can access your account in advance.
schedule:
Yamashita Park
5/21 (Thu) - 5/24 (Sun)
Thursday 16:00-21:00
Friday & Saturday 11:00-21:00
Sunday 11:00-20:00
(last order 30min before closing time)
description:
Enjoy Belgian Beer Weekend!




★ Enjoy your beer in the official BBW glass! ★
To enjoy your Belgian beers at their best, we only serve in the official Belgian Beer Weekend glass — not plastic. The glass is yours to keep as a souvenir of your BBW experience.




★ Pay with tokens! ★
At the beer and food stalls, payment is accepted by tokens only. Tokens can be purchased at the event ticket counters, where various payment methods are available.
> 1 token = ¥230
Soft drinks: 1 token
Beer: from 4 tokens
Food: from 2 tokens




★ Rinse your glass between drinks! ★
To ensure every beer tastes its best, we provide glass rinsers throughout the venue. Simply rinse your glass before your next beer for a fresh pour every time.

title: Hida Great Limestone Cave & Ohashi Collection Museum Advance Tickets [100 JPY Discount VS Same-Day Tickets Onsite]
overview:
The Hida Great Limestone Cave lies between Hida Takayama and Okuhida-Onsengo in Gifu Prefecture. Totaling about 800 meters in length, these limestone caves simply abound with sights to see and marvel at.




At an altitude of 900 meters, it is the highest tourist limestone cave in Japan. Temperatures in the cave are stable year-round, averaging a cool 12°C, making it a great retreat from the summer heat!




Visitors can also enjoy the Ohashi Collection Museum, displaying some 1,000 works of art and decorative objects from around the world.




Buy tickets from Rakuten Travel Experiences to get 100 JPY off for adults (high school students and older): 1,000 JPY vs 1,100 JPY for same-day tickets sold onsite, and for children (elementary school and junior high school students): 450 JPY vs 550 JPY for same-day tickets sold onsite.
highlights: Pets are also welcome
You can visit the Ohashi Collection Museum on the same trip
Explore a mystery that Mother Nature created over ~250 million years
At an altitude of 900 meters, it is the highest tourist limestone cave in the country
Temperatures in the cave are stable year-round averaging a cool 12°C, making it a choice retreat from the summer heat!
important_info:
.
In order to preserve the natural environment there is no elevator, therefore the site is not wheelchair-accessible.
.
If you plan to drive to the site during winter snowfall, please use studless winter tires or chains.
.
Please note that opening hours vary depending on the season.
.
The Hida Great Limestone Cave is divided into three sections from Cave 1 to Cave 3.
Each section has its own exits. The sections after Exit 2 have steep pathways, so if you have any concerns about your stamina or lower-body strength, we recommend leaving the cave via Exit 1 or 2.
.
Approximate time needed from the Limestone Cave entrance to Exit 1 is 10 minutes. To Exit 2 is 20 minutes and to Exit 3 is 30 minutes. From Exit 3 to the parking lot is about an 8-minute walk (all times are rough estimates).
.
Please click the link in the voucher sent after the purchase to open the website page, press the "Use" button, and present the screen to the staff.
*Please make sure that your smartphone connects to the internet as you will need to launch the website from your smartphone.
*Please note that screenshots or printed-out tickets will not be acceptable.
.
Free parking available.
.
Discounts are available for the disabled. Please present your disability certificate (physical disability certificate or rehabilitation certificate) when you purchase tickets onsite. An accompanying person can receive a 100 JPY discount and pay 1,000 JPY (multiple tickets are available).
meeting_point:
Meeting point: Hida Great Limestone Cave & Ohashi Collection Museum

Address: 1147 Hiyomo, Nyukawacho, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture, 506-2256
Access: 30 minutes by car from Takayama IC on the Chubu Jukan Expressway



Business Hours

description:
Highlights of the Hida Great Limestone Cave:




• Ryuuguu No Ya Kei — Cave 1
(Nightscape of the Dragon's Palace)
This surreal scene greets you upon entering the Hida Great Limestone Cave. It is deliberately lit with red and blue lights to prevent moss from growing.




• Dream Palace — Cave 2
The icicle-like soda-straw stalactites are one of the trademark features of the Great Hida Limestone Cave and can be seen here in large quantities.




• Aishin Spot — Cave 2
An underground tunnel that connects Cave 1 to Cave 2. Colorful LED lights stage a mystical atmosphere along the way.




• Tsuki No Sekai — Cave 3
(Moon World)
Formed of cave coral, the dramatic cleft in the rock is a sight to behold! But beware — the pathway to this spot is very narrow.




Ohashi Collection Museum:




An art museum established to complement the Hida Great Limestone Cave. Around 1,000 artworks and decorative items collected from around the world are on display here. The Ohashi Collection Museum is located in front of the Limestone Cave entrance — be sure to take a look on your visit to the cave!
how_it_works:
April–October 08:00–17:00 (closed 17:30)
November–March 09:00–16:00 (closed 16:30)




In order to preserve the natural environment there is no elevator, therefore the site is not wheelchair-accessible.

title: Instant Universal Studios Japan Studio Pass Osaka [E-Ticket]
overview:
Prepare for non-stop excitement with same-day Universal Studios Japan instant e-tickets! Buy a Universal Studio Pass from Rakuten, and prepare to explore amazing themed zones like The Wizarding World of Harry Potter™ and Super Nintendo World™.




Book a Universal Express Pass as well, to skip lines for popular rides. USJ tickets are not sold on-site, so booking from an authorized partner like Rakuten Travel Experiences is a must!




New for 2026 — order popcorn cups online in advance! To avoid disappointment, book a Popcorn Cup Voucher (likely to sell out) for up to 3 refills on the day (popcorn buckets sold separately).
highlights: Use your Rakuten account to earn or use Rakuten Points for each ticket!
Book online to save time on the day!
Maximize your time with Universal Express Pass (sold separately) to skip the lines at popular rides
Explore 10 amazing areas like Super Nintendo World™ and The Wizarding World of Harry Potter™
description:
Click here to check the right pass type for your day of visit!




With a Studio Pass ticket for Universal Studios Japan in Osaka, explore 10 amazing themed zones, including Super Nintendo World™ and The Wizarding World of Harry Potter™. Enjoy thrill rides like the Flying Dinosaur and interactive experiences like Mario Kart: Koopa's Challenge!







And there’s always something new at Universal Studios Japan!




Super Nintendo World™ PLAY WILD! Let your passion run wild!




Immerse yourself in the world of Mario and friends and use your whole body to have fun! And now Donkey Kong has arrived! Anything can happen in his jungle and that means thrills and exhilaration. Before you know it, you'll be all caught up in the excitement, body and soul!




© Nintendo




This is the place in the world where kids and grown-ups alike can return to the wild and play all out with Mario and Donkey Kong! The magic word is "Play wild!" Awaken your instinct to play!




Mario Kart: Koopa's Challenge: Immerse yourself in a real-world Mario Kart experience!




© Nintendo




Yoshi's Adventure: Hop on Yoshi for an adorable adventure!

Minions and all related elements and indicia TM & © 2024 Universal Studios. All rights reserved.




Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey™: You can experience the magic like never before… An upgraded version of the beloved adventure ride brings the thrill of a new dimension.




HARRY POTTER and all related characters and elements © & ™ Warner Bros. Entertainment Inc. Publishing Rights © J.K. Rowling.




The Flying Dinosaur: Soar like a bird!




TM & © Universal Studios & Amblin Entertainment




• Photos are for illustrative purposes only
• Information on Universal Studios Japan is as of August 19, 2024, and subject to change




TM & © Universal Studios. All rights reserved

how_it_works:

• Depending on crowd conditions, distribution of “Area Timed Entry” may end ahead of schedule. On the other hand, you may be able to enter an area without an “Area Timed Entry Ticket: Advance Booking,” or "Area Timed Entry Ticket/Standby Entry Ticket" in some cases.
• To ensure the best experience, guests should either purchase a Universal Express Pass or an Area Timed Entry Ticket in advance.
• Download the Universal Studios Japan official app in advance to apply for an “Area Timed Entry Ticket,” and for an even smoother park experience!
• Please refer to the Universal Studios Japan official website for more information.
• To provide a healthy and safe environment to all guests, Universal Studios Japan adopts advanced sanitation measures in accordance with the "Theme Park Infection Prevention Guidelines;" for details, visit the official website.
• Attractions, restaurants, shops, and photo services may be closed or operations suspended without prior notice.
• Contents or hours of events, attractions, and facilities are subject to change or cancellation depending on season, time, or weather, without notice.
• For operating hours, please visit the official site.

title: Osaka Umekita Onsen REN Wellbeing Park E-Ticket"
overview:
Umekita Onsen REN Wellbeing Park is an urban-style hot spring facility newly opened in the Umeda area of Osaka.




With various kinds of baths, saunas, and the latest health and wellness programs, it offers a relaxing space to rejuvenate both body and mind.




Designed in harmony with a lush green park, it provides a wellbeing experience where you can enjoy nature while still being in the heart of the city.
highlights: Enjoy a full day with excellent facilities including various kinds of baths, saunas, and a pool!
important_info:
・Guests with tattoos are not permitted to enter the facility. However, fashion tattoos that can be completely covered using up to four of our designated cover stickers (11.4cm × 20cm, available for purchase) are allowed if covered properly.
・Guests must be 12 years of age or older to enter. Children under 12 are not allowed in any area of the facility. Guests under 18 must be accompanied by an adult over the age of 18 (excluding high school students).
・All package prices include towel rental, indoor wear, and amenities.
・You may stay for up to 12 hours with any of our admission plans.
・If you stay beyond 12 hours from the time of entry, an additional fee of 2,700 (2,970) yen will be automatically charged.
・If your stay exceeds 24 hours, you must settle your bill and exit the facility.
・Re-entry is not permitted.
・Participation in paid programs and the use of premium seating will incur additional charges.
・The indoor pool is free to use. It operates as a fitness pool until 8:00 PM, and as a leisure pool after 8:00 PM. The switch-over time may vary depending on the day or season.
・On designated peak days (such as New Year holidays, Golden Week, and Obon), weekend/holiday rates will apply.
・Access to the stone sauna, lava sauna, and the outdoor heated infinity pool requires an additional fee.
・The facility is open year-round, but may close temporarily for maintenance or inspections. Please check the official website before your visit.
・The outdoor pool included in the 1-Day Passport Plan requires an additional fee of 3,000 yen (tax included: 3,300 yen) during the summer season (July 1 – September 30).
・A late-night fee of 1,000 yen (1,100 yen including tax) applies to stays between 12:00 AM and 5:00 AM.
*Please note that we may ask for proof of age if you arrive between 12:00 AM and 5:00 AM.
《1st Anniversary Campaign》 The 1,000 (1,100) yen late-night fee is waived!

title: MIRACLEWORLD Admission Pass
overview:
Osaka’s first permanent indoor digital art facility has opened in Namba.
Step underground and immerse yourself in a world surrounded by 360° LED visuals and mirror-reflected immersive projections, where an entirely new realm unfolds before your eyes.




In the interactive zone, the visuals change in response to each visitor’s movements and interests, allowing you to fully experience a fusion of reality and creativity through interactive art that engages all your senses.




MIRACLE WORLD is a fantastical space where art, imagery, and technology come together.
An entirely new experience awaits you here, one that can only be found in this place.
Escape from the ordinary and step into a world beyond reality.
Discover an unseen world before your eyes through your own senses and imagination.
highlights: Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
important_info:
.
Children aged 12 and under must be accompanied by a parent or guardian aged 18 or older.
.
Children aged 3 and under are free of charge and do not require a ticket.
.
Animals are not allowed, except for assistance dogs.
.
Please present your ticket with a QR code (on your smartphone screen or printed) for admission.
.
Admission is limited to the reserved time slot only.
.
Once you leave the venue, re-entry is not permitted.
.
Resale for commercial purposes, as well as listing or transferring tickets on auction sites or flea market apps, is strictly prohibited.
.
In the event that resale is discovered, the relevant ticket will be rendered invalid, and admission may be refused.
.
【Regarding Items Brought into the Venue】
・Please use the designated parking area for strollers.
・Large luggage cannot be brought into the venue.
（There is no storage facility inside the building, so please use external lockers in advance.）
・Food and drinks cannot be brought in (bottled drinks with lids and water bottles are allowed). Alcohol and hazardous items are not permitted.
・Please refrain from wearing clothing or bringing items that may disturb other guests’ viewing experience, such as light-up shoes or glow sticks.
.
【Regarding Viewing】
・Some exhibits include dark areas or uneven footing.
・Children must be supervised and guided safely under the responsibility of their guardians.
・Due to structural limitations, some exhibits may not be accessible.
・Children must always be supervised by their guardians to ensure safe viewing.
・Wheelchair entry is possible.
（However, some exhibits may not be accessible due to structural limitations.）
・Some exhibits feature mirrored floors.
（A wrap cloth is available for guests wearing skirts.）
Please view the exhibits within your personal comfort and safety limits if any of the following apply
・Those who have experienced seizures or loss of consciousness triggered by light stimuli
・Those who are extremely uncomfortable with confined, dark, or high places
・Those who have concerns about their physical condition or stamina
・Those who are prone to motion sickness-like symptoms caused by visual or light effects (similar to VR sickness)
.
[Regarding Photography]
・Photography and video recording are allowed, but please be considerate of other visitors.
・The use of tripods, selfie sticks, or any equipment longer than 30 cm is not permitted.
・Commercial photography or media coverage requires prior approval.
.
[Other Information]
・Please arrive and line up before your designated admission time.
・Some exhibits may be unavailable due to maintenance or other reasons.
・Exhibit contents are subject to change or cancellation without notice.
・The organizer is not responsible for any injuries, losses, or troubles that may occur inside the venue.
・For your safety, please follow the instructions of the staff.

title: Dejima Admission E-ticket in Nagasaki　This is a place you definitely can't miss when sightseeing in Nagasaki.
overview: Travel back in time to Dejima, a historic island built in 1636 has served as Japan’s only window to the Western world and it has played a vital role in Japan’s modernization for 218 years during the Sakoku (national isolation) period . In the Meiji era Dejima lost the original shape of the island due to the land reclamation. In 1951 Nagasaki City began restoration work and today 16 buildings and landscapes have been faithfully restored, offering visitors an immersive glimpse into this significant era.
highlights: Book with a Rakuten account to earn and use Rakuten Points for every ticket!
important_info:
E-tickets are valid only on the selected date and cannot be changed or canceled.
Tickets are available for groups of 1–14 people. For 15 or more, group discounts are offered onsite. (※Note: until March 31, 2026)
Re-entry is not permitted.
Entrance is currently only from the Dejima Omotemon Bridge.
Some areas may be closed for maintenance.
Complimentary wheelchair rental is available (up to 7 units). Please inquire at the Dejima Information Center (Omotemon Gate).
Smoking is strictly prohibited within Dejima and surrounding areas as per Nagasaki City regulations.



About Discounted Admission
For details, please check the official website and contact the Dejima Management Office directly to apply.
what_to_bring:
・ A mobile device with internet connection to redeem your e-ticket/voucher.
click here for how to use.

Operating Days: Open year-round

Hours: 08:00–21:00 (Last entry: 20:40)

The Nagasaki International Club Dejima Restaurant: 11:00–18:00
- Lunch: 13:45 last order
- Tea Time: 17:00 last order
- Dinner: Reservations only after 18:00
Dejima Museum Shop: 09:00–18:00




Note: Please check the official website for the latest updates before your visit.
description:
Schedule



Operating Days: Open year-round
Hours: 08:00–21:00 (Last entry: 20:40)
**The Nagasaki International Club Restaurant:**11:00–18:00(Dinner is by reservation only)
Dejima Museum Shop: 09:00–18:00



Highlights of Restored Buildings



First Ship Captain’s Quarters
Once home to Dutch ship captains and Dutch employees, this building showcases the living spaces furnished with the beds and tables on its second floor, while the first floor replicates the warehouse.







Deputy Factor's Quarters
This building served as the home of the deputy chief factor of the Dutch Trading Post. The first floor is now a museum shop offering “Dejima goods,” while the second floor is a kimono rental shop "Dejima Hoppen."
access:
From JR Nagasaki Station:



By Bus: Take Nagasaki Bus #17 or #27 from Nagasaki Station South Exit. Get off at Dejima and walk 1 minute.
By Tram: From Nagasaki Station, take the tram for Sofukuji Temple and get off at Dejima (4-minute walk). Alternatively, alight at Shinchi Chinatown and walk 4 minutes.
By Car: About 6 minutes from JR Nagasaki Station or 1 minute from Nagasaki IC via the Nagasaki Dejima Road. No parking lot on Dejima. Please use a pay parking lot nearby.



From Nagasaki Airport:



By Nagasaki Bus or Nagasaki Ken-ei Bus:

Via Dejima Road: Approx. 43 minutes (approx. 35 minutes to Shinchi Chinatown)
Via Showa-machi: Approx. 55 minutes.
Fare: 1,200 JPY (adults), 600 JPY (children under 12) one-way.
how_it_works: Your e-ticket requires an internet connection to use (click here for how to use)

title: Factory tour of giant robot “INGRAM” in Tokyo [PATLABOR LAB]
overview:
You can feel like you are the main character of the anime by visiting INGRAM, the 5 meters class giant robot that appears in the anime.




Step into the world of Mobile Police Patlabor at the facility in Tokyo where you can find INGRAM. Explore our human-operated robot research and development facility!




STORY
The AVX-S30 INGRAM (prototype) has been through numerous tests to collect data for its 128-bit CPU upgrade. By gathering piloting data from people all over the
world, Ingram is learning to move more smoothly and effectively!
highlights: Buy Patlabor merchandise sold only in the Patlabor Lab.
Learn about the anime Patlabor and the story behind development of INGRAM.
Viewing tour of the giant mecha, INGRAM, in Tokyo, Japan.
Watch and take photos with the full-scale INGRAM from the anime.
See the cutting-edge robotic technologies.
important_info:
[Not allowed]
・Pets
・Swimwear
・Smoking indoors
・Alcohol and drugs
・See-through clothing
[To know before you go]
・Please be sure to be on time, the time slot can't be extended for late arrivals.
・The legs of the robot have been removed to fit into the lab.
・Pilot must complete a safety agreement form provided in the lab before operating the robot.
・The operation experience, which costs an additional fee, is approximately 10 minutes per person.
・Follow all instructions and operate safety.
・Avoid mischievous behavior and adhere to staff guidance.
・Individuals with severe physical conditions or illnesses are not permitted to operate the robot.
・While the safety measures are taken, all visitors must have overseas insurance in case of injury.
・Wearing a costume is allowed as long as the outfit matches the Patlabor theme, but outfits that excessively expose skin are prohibited.
・If you wish to extend your stay, there is an additional fee.
description:
Date & Time: Monday, Wednesday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (11:00-19:00)
Maximum number of participants: 5 people
Address: Taihei 1-17-6 20H Sumida city Tokyo [MOVeLOT PATLABOR LAB]




Other experiences available for additional fee on site
・Pilot the INGRAM （If there is no pilot）
・Sit in INGRAM’s cockpit and enjoy the seat elevation experience — just like in the anime!




[Schedule for the Day]
・Get security pass
・Watch INGRAM startup
・View full-scale INGRAM and take photos
・Hear about Ingram's aircraft
・Photograph with full-scale revolver cannon
・Fill out a report, view and photograph exhibits, and purchase limited-edition merchandise
*You can also board the cockpit and experience the ascent and descent of the aircraft in its motionless state with an optional extra!
how_it_works:
Using tickets: your e-ticket voucher requires an internet connection to use, and should only be redeemed by staff.
